heading: Transportation of dependents and household effects of civilian personnel stationed outside the United States: payment in lieu of transportation
label: 8747 Transportation of dependents and household effects of civilian personnel stationed outside the United States: payment in lieu of transportation
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)7477.37 U.S.C. 112c(3d par.), ; , .Feb. 18, 1946, ch. 3060 Stat. 20Oct. 12, 1949, ch. 681, § 52463 Stat. 836In subsection (a) the words “without regard to rank or grade” and “or subsequent to the discharge or release of such personnel from active service” are omitted as applicable only to members of the naval service. The Act of , , applied to both civilian and naval personnel and was amended by the , by deleting therefrom all reference to naval personnel. children.text: from the locations outside the United States to locations designated by such personnel or their dependents; and
from those designated locations to the duty stations to which the personnel are ordered.

children.label: b
children.text: Authority to transport household effects under this section includes authority to pack and unpack those effects.

children.label: c
children.text: Transportation of dependents and household effects is authorized under this section either before or after orders are issued relieving the civilian concerned from the duty station outside the United States. The transportation may be by Government or commercial facilities.

children.label: d
children.text: In place of the transportation in kind authorized for dependents, the Secretary may authorize the payment, after the travel has been completed, of an amount equal to the commercial transportation costs, including taxes if paid, of all parts of the travel for which transportation in kind was not furnished.

children.label: e
children.text: Current appropriations available for travel and transportation may be used for expenditures under this section.
